violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with food residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed it in warewashing area to be washed, rinsed and sanitized. Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025

Cheese was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved product to walk-in cooler. Facility shall not use sandwich cold holding unit for TCS food until unit is serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025
Observed ice buildup in walk-in freezer. Ice shall be broken down and removed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

Observed the sandwich cold holding unit located in the prep/kitchen area was not in good repair/needs adjustment. Sandwich cold holding unit's ambient temperature was between 46-47 degrees F. Per interview with PIC and temperature logs the unit was 36 degrees prior to inspection. PIC relocated all TCS foods into walk-in cooler. Per PIC unit will be serviced today/tomorrow. New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2024
